category: Tyrosine kinase inhibitor (BCR-ABL; also FLT3 activity)
mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and other kinases; has reported activity against FLT3-driven signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; listed test range was 0.3 nM–3 µM (lower bound converted to µM).

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family kinases and ABL to disrupt signaling required for proliferation in some AML subsets
additional_notes: Reported activity in subsets and in combinations; listed test range was 0.3 nM–10 µM (lower bound converted to µM).

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway, reducing downstream ERK signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Approved in melanoma; positioned as AML signaling-node inhibitor; listed test range was 0.3 nM–3 µM (lower bound converted to µM).

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, increasing histone acetylation and altering transcription to impair AML cell growth
additional_notes: Approved in multiple myeloma; listed test range was 0.3 nM–1 µM (lower bound converted to µM).

mechanism: At higher concentrations exerts HDAC-inhibitory activity affecting gene expression and differentiation
additional_notes: Clinically used anticonvulsant; HDAC activity noted as occurring at high concentrations.

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, disrupting protein degradation and inducing proteotoxic stress/apoptosis
Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, causing accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved in multiple myeloma; listed test range was 0.3 nM–1 µM (lower bound converted to µM).

mechanism: Induces oxidative stress and apoptosis; classically targets PML-RARA in APL and can affect broader AML contexts
additional_notes: Approved in APL; broader AML activity noted as context-dependent.

mechanism: Activates AMPK and inhibits mitochondrial complex I, altering cellular metabolism and energy stress responses
additional_notes: Epidemiology and preclinical AML data mentioned.

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and impairs autophagosome-lysosome function, inhibiting autophagy and sensitizing cells to stress
additional_notes: Text lists “Chloroquine or Hydroxychloroquine” as alternatives; combos implied.

mechanism: Inhibits ALDH and can promote ROS-mediated cytotoxicity, often enhanced by copper
additional_notes: Copper-dependent cytotoxicity noted; tip to include ±1–5 µM CuCl2 to test copper dependence; listed test range was 10 nM–30 µM (lower bound converted to µM).
